Empires & Puzzles Mother North Review: The Original Revive Queen

Mother North was once the most feared hero in Empires & Puzzles, snatching victory from the jaws of defeat with a single spoon. But with aging stats and a flashy new costume, does the original porridge still hold up? We break down her revival mechanics, survival rates, and why she's still a Rush War nightmare.
Mother North Review: Is The Queen of Christmas Still Relevant?
Mother North: The Granny Who Refuses to Let You Die
Mother North is a 5-star Nature hero from the

(Santa's Challenge). Released way back in 2018, she holds the title of the original "rage-quit" inducer. While newer heroes boast complex mechanics and paragraphs of text, Mother North keeps it simple: she brings your dead team back to life.

. While her Costume gives her a trendy makeover (and much-needed stats), the original Cleric version still has a dedicated following, especially among those who prefer instant gratification (healing) over patience.

The original Mother North artwork is iconic. She’s the quintessential warm, welcoming grandmother figure, armed with a ladle that looks like it could double as a blunt weapon. It's a cozy, traditional design that screams "comfort food," which ironically contrasts with the absolute panic she causes when you see her charging up on defense. She looks like she's about to feed you cookies, but she's actually feeding your opponents a second chance at life.

This is incredibly low. For context, modern 3-star heroes are pushing 550+ attack. She hits like a wet noodle, but thankfully, her job isn't to deal damage.
Respectable for her time, but now considered "squishy" compared to modern tanks boasting 1300+ defense.
This is the danger zone. Modern snipers can wipe this out in one hit.

Without her Costume Bonus or heavy Limit Breaking, Base Mother North is extremely fragile in today's meta. She relies entirely on her class talent (Manashield) and minions to survive long enough to fire.

A coin flip. When it works, it's a miracle. When it fails, it's heartbreaking. But 50% is a very generous roll in a game where RNG often feels rigged.

Base Mother North does not have innate passives like modern heroes (e.g., "Resist Block"). She relies entirely on her
is the perfect Aether Power for her. Since her only goal is to survive long enough to cast "Super Porridge," any extra mitigation is gold.

Upon release, Mother North broke the game. She was the premier Rush Attack and Rush Defense hero. People famously pulled hundreds of times just for her. She was part of the "Christmas Defense" nightmare (Mother North - Krampus - Santa/Pengi) that dominated wars for years.
Her revive mechanic hasn't aged; 50% is still 50%. However, her
has plummeted. In an era of heroes hitting for 600% damage with bypass, 819 Defense doesn't cut it.
. In standard speed raids, she is often too slow and too fragile to fire before being sniped. However, seeing her in the corner of a Rush defense still induces anxiety.

The Costume version is generally superior due to the stat increase and the fact that the HoT heals the revived allies. However, the Base version's Cleric talent (Manashield) is arguably better than Monk for a slow healer, as it stops mana stops/cuts.

. Even with outdated stats, a 5* reviver is a game-changer for completing map stages and quests that are otherwise too hard for your roster. She will carry you through Season 2, 3, and 4 Hard modes.
She is likely relegated to your Rush War defense or a backup War Attack team. If you have the Costume, you're likely using that side. If you
have the base, she needs LB2 to survive in top-tier wars.
"She’s the only Christmas hero I don’t have. Hoping to get her this time around." -
"Once I almost had her dead, but she simultaneously revived three of her four allies... I lost." -
"Revives have saved me more times than any single special in my roster."
The community consensus has always been that Mother North is the "Safety Net" of E&P. Even years later, that sentiment holds true.

Mother North is like that old cast-iron skillet in your kitchen: she might not look as shiny as the new non-stick ceramic pans (modern heroes), but she gets the job done and lasts forever. While her stats are showing their age, her ability to completely flip a losing battle with a single cast makes her timeless.
If you hear "Porridge!" in a raid, pray to the RNG gods.
Is Mother North still worth it in 2026? We analyze her stats, special skill, and viability in the modern meta compared to her costume.
